data.title: Millrose Properties forecasts Q3 revenue above consensus estimate
data.description:
Millrose Properties (NYSE:MRP) announced on Thursday preliminary third-quarter revenue of ~$179.3M, above the consensus estimate of $167.8M.

Shares were +0.58% pre-market to $32.88.

Revenue includes option fee revenue and development loan income.

AFFO per share is estimated at $0.74 for the quarter, compared to the consensus of $0.72.

Net income is expected to be about $105.1M, homesite inventory and other related assets at about $8.7B, invested capital of ~$8.2B, and book value of approximately $35.29 per share.

The company plans to release its quarterly results before the market opens on October 23.

data.title: OpenAI's Sora poses risk to creators' rights, says Hollywood talent agency CAA: report
data.description:
Hollywood talent agency Creative Artists Agency said that OpenAI (OPENAI) is exposing artists to "significant risk" through its new AI video-generating tool Sora, Reuters reported.

"The question is, does OpenAI and its partner companies believe that humans, writers, artists, actors, directors, producers, musicians, and athletes deserve to be compensated and credited for the work they create?" said CAA said in a statement to the news agency, according to the report.

OpenAI did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Sora lets users create and share short AI videos which can be spun from copyrighted content and shared to social media-like streams.

Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT)-backed OpenAI will soon unveil controls allowing the owners of content rights to decide how their characters are used in Sora, and plans to share revenue with those who permit such use, Chief Executive Sam Altman posted on his blog on Friday.

The Los Angeles-based CAA represents thousands of actors, directors and music artists and athletes.

Disney (NYSE:DIS) has opted out of having its material appear in the app, people familiar with the matter told the news agency.

CAA said control, permission for use, and compensation were "a fundamental right" of creative workers and warned the misuse of new technologies poses "serious and harmful risks" that extended beyond the entertainment and media industries.

The agency noted that it was open to hearing OpenAI's solutions to these issues and was working with intellectual property businesses, creative guilds and unions, and legislators and policymakers to address the challenges.

data.title: Alibaba signs AI, cloud deal with NBA China
data.description:
Alibaba (NYSE:BABA) announced on Thursday that it has signed a multi-year with NBA China to develop artificial-intelligence-backed applications to boost live game viewing and other fan engagement experiences.

The announcement comes ahead of the NBA's return to China, where it had been gone for six years. Some NBA teams, including the Brooklyn Nets, will play regular season games on Friday and Sunday in Macau, the Asian gambling destination. The Brooklyn Nets are owned by Alibaba Chairman Joe Tsai.

“It is an exciting time at the intersection of sports and technology,” Tsai said in a statement. “AI and cloud technologies are enablers of connection—between player and fan, brand and consumer, global vision and local culture. By collaborating with NBA China and introducing Alibaba’s latest innovations, we are here to excite NBA fans through the enablement of exceptional experiences that they’ve never seen before.”

As part of the deal, NBA China will create an AI model that is based on Alibaba's Qwen series of foundation models. The Qwen family of models has helped spur a rally in Alibaba's share price and are partly responsible for the company announcing a massive boost to its AI-related spending last month.

Other aspects of the deal include Alibaba Cloud debuting a real-time 360 replay solution for the NBA China games; and NBA China using Alibaba Cloud's infrastructure to host its digital destinations, including the NBA app, the league’s official website in China and NBA China mini-programs.

NBA China will also use Alibaba Cloud’s intelligent analytical tools, while Alibaba's consumer facing apps, including its Quark and Tongyi apps, will become official marketing partners of the NBA China. 

Alibaba shares fell 1.2% in premarket trading on Thursday.

data.title: Archrock announces redemption of all outstanding 6.875% senior notes
data.description:
• Archrock (NYSE:AROC) stated that a wholly owned subsidiary of the company plans to redeem all $300M aggregate principal amount of its outstanding 6.875% senior notes due 2027.
  • The notes will be redeemed on November 17 at a redemption price equal to 100% of their principal amount plus accrued and unpaid interest.

data.title: JPMorgan CEO Dimon says U.S. stock market could be headed for serious correction-report
data.description:
The U.S. stock market could be at high risk of a serious correction within the next six months to two years, according to JPMorgan (JPM) CEO Jamie Dimon. 

Dimon, in an interview with BBC, added that the U.S. has become a "less reliable" partner on the global platform and that he was still a little worried about the inflation in the country. 

However, Dimon emphasized that the Federal Reserve should remain independent. This comes amid President Donald Trump's strong criticism towards Fed Chair Jerome Powell and concerns about the autonomy of the central bank. 

The bank's chief believes that there are a lot of things that are leading to an environment of uncertainty, including geopolitical risks, fiscal spending and the remilitarization of the world.

"All these things cause a lot of issues that we don't know how to answer. So I say the level of uncertainty should be higher in most people's minds than what I would call normal," he said in the interview. 

Dimon held an overall positive view on artificial intelligence, one of the main drivers of the stock market rally in recent times. "The way I look at it is AI is real, AI in total will pay off," he said. 

Since the start of the year, the S&P 500 (SP500) is up 14.8%, Dow (DJI) has risen 9.5%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) has risen 19.3%. 

Stock index futures were almost flat on Thursday, as a tech rally on Wednesday pushed the Nasdaq Composite to another record high, while investors looked forward to Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell's welcome remarks at the Community Bank Conference. 

Here are some exchange-traded funds that track the S&P 500: (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

data.title: Ørsted to cut 25% of jobs amid strategic shift in renewable energy
data.description:
Danish renewable energy giant Ørsted (OTCPK:DNNGY) (OTCPK:DOGEF) plans to reduce its global workforce by roughly a quarter as it narrows its focus to core offshore and European markets.

The company said about 2,000 roles will be eliminated by 2027, trimming its current headcount of around 8,000. The first round of reductions -- roughly 500 jobs, including 235 in Denmark -- will take place before the end of this year.

Chief Executive Rasmus Errboe said the downsizing reflects both the completion of several major projects and a sharpened market strategy.

“We want to create a more efficient and flexible organisation and a more competitive Ørsted, ready to bid on new value-accretive offshore wind projects,” he said in a statement.

Errboe added that cutting costs across development, construction and operations is critical to keeping Ørsted competitive in offshore wind.

The job cuts will be achieved through attrition, divestments, outsourcing and layoffs.

The announcement follows Ørsted’s $9.4 billion equity raise earlier this week, intended to support ongoing offshore wind construction at a time when the industry is contending with rising costs and financial pressure.

data.title: Assurant acquires OptoFidelity’s mobile device testing solutions
data.description:
• Assurant (NYSE:AIZ) said on Thursday that it has acquired the portfolio of mobile device test automation technology from OptoFidelity, a provider of advanced optical metrology systems for mobile devices. 
  • OptoFidelity’s technology improves operational efficiency and further strengthens Assurant’s ability to scale, optimize resources, and easily integrate with existing processes to support future innovation. 
  • Founded in 2005, OptoFidelity produces advanced mobile device testing systems known for precision, repeatability, and traceability. 
  • In the 20 years since its founding, the company has delivered over 9,000 test systems to mobile carriers, manufacturers, and retailers worldwide.

data.title: Neogen to cut 10% of workforce; shares rise
data.description:
• Neogen (NASDAQ:NEOG) on Thursday said it would cut 10% of its workforce to reduce costs after reaffirming its full-year outlook, a move that lifted shares.
  • Targeted operational improvements to gross margin, focusing on core process execution to drive improvement in inventory, Neogen (NASDAQ:NEOG) said.
  • The update comes after the company reaffirmed its full-year outlook for fiscal year 2026.
  • Neogen (NEOG) +10% at $6.40 in premarket trading.

data.title: CDT Equity announces 1-for-8 reverse stock split effective October 10
data.description:
• CDT Equity (NASDAQ:CDT) to implement a 1-for-8 reverse stock split effective October 10, 2025.
  • Trading on a split-adjusted basis will begin on October 13, 2025.
  • Purpose of the split: potential to raise more funds to support the company’s strategy.
  • Outstanding shares will be reduced to ~1.35M post-split.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Industrial Logistics Properties declares $0.05 dividend
data.description:
• Industrial Logistics Properties (NASDAQ:ILPT) declared $0.05/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 3.65%
  • Payable Nov. 13; for shareholders of record Oct. 27; ex-div Oct. 27.
  • See ILPT Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.
